pattern: Partition cost is additive, and within one piece a swap only pays off when it fixes two mirrored mismatches, so a quadratic DP over split points wins.
hints: Let dp[i] be the cheapest way to match the first i characters; it comes from a previous split plus one substring's cost.
For one substring, optionally reverse it, then a swap is worth using only when two positions hold each other's needed letters; everything else is a replace.
Within a piece, cost = optional reverse + mismatches minus the number of mutually-fixable swap pairs; take the better of reversed and not.

stress.description: short strings over a tiny alphabet so the partition oracle stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 7)))
    import string
    alpha = string.ascii_lowercase[:4]
    w1 = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    w2 = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    return [w1, w2]
